title: cynoglossum creticum var. siculum
intro: Cynoglossum creticum var. siculum, also known as the Sicilian Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The health benefits of Cynoglossum creticum var. siculum include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
